definition: Central Bank Dealers Range — the price range formed between 2:00 PM and 8:00 PM New York time. This is when institutional algorithms reset and set the next day's reference range.
depends_on: sessions_and_kill_zones (CBDR occurs between NY close and Asian open)
feeds_into: daily_bias (CBDR range direction hints at next day bias)
venom_model (Venom uses pre-market range which extends CBDR concept)
related_to: power_of_three_and_AMD (CBDR IS pre-accumulation — the reset before Asian accumulation)
asian_range (Asian range typically contains or extends CBDR)

bridge_integration.trending_conviction: CBDR width < 30% of ADR = tight range = compression = trending day expected. Assign higher conviction (+10 points) to directional trades.
bridge_integration.ranging_conviction: CBDR width > 60% of ADR = wide CBDR = already distributed = ranging day likely. Reduce position size, lower conviction.
bridge_integration.sweep_direction: Always confirm which side of CBDR was swept first (London manipulation). The sweep direction tells you which 1 SD projection is the false move and which is the real target.
bridge_integration.adr_comparison: ADR = Average Daily Range (20-day). CBR/ADR ratio is the key filter for day type classification.

common_mistakes: Using CBDR projections without confirming the sweep direction with London or NY price action. The projection alone does not tell you which direction — the SWEEP tells you direction, the SD levels tell you targets.

time_window.context: Occurs after the New York session closes and before the Asian session opens. This is the transition window where institutional desks reset their reference ranges for the next 24-hour cycle.

CBR_calculation.definition: The Central Bank Range (CBR) is the pip/point distance between the highest and lowest price print during the 2 PM - 8 PM ET window.
CBR_calculation.purpose: CBR becomes the unit of measurement for standard deviation projections. It represents the baseline volatility expectation for the next session.

SD_projections.definition: Standard deviation projections above and below the CBDR range provide probable target zones for the next session.

probability.1_SD: 68% probability price reaches this level — primary target zone
probability.2_SD: 95% probability boundary — strong reversal zone if reached

asian_range_relationship.definition: The Asian session (8 PM - midnight ET) runs immediately after CBDR. Its behavior relative to CBDR defines the next day's type.
asian_range_relationship.contained: Asian range stays within CBDR boundaries — confirms tight institutional range, compression building. Expect a larger London/NY move.
asian_range_relationship.breaks_one_side: Asian range extends beyond one CBDR boundary — that extension is the manipulation side. London often reverses from Asian extreme.
asian_range_relationship.breaks_both_sides: Rare. Signals high volatility session ahead. CBDR projections become less reliable.

day_type_rules.trending_day: If London breaks ABOVE CBDR High + 1 SD, the day is likely trending bullish. Look for continuation entries on pullbacks to 1 SD level.
day_type_rules.ranging_day: If London/NY price remains CONTAINED within 1 SD of CBDR, a ranging day is expected. Fade extremes, target the opposite CBDR boundary.
day_type_rules.reversal_signal: If price reaches 2 SD during London, expect mean reversion toward CBDR range during NY session.
